title: Arlong’s Rule Tightens Around Nami
scene: On pages 4–7, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. Usopp arrives ahead of the others and gets pulled into the fear surrounding Arlong’s domain.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: There it is!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I found going merry!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: She docked it over there!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hey, what's the meaning of this! Until me now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Hmm, that certainly is a rather strange place to dock the ship.

dialogue.speaker: Cocoyasi villagers
dialogue.text: It's a little off from the "Cocoyashi Village" marked on this map.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I said untie me!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: That's enough. Look at yourself, you're about to faint just from yelling!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: You were an inch away from death, remember?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Just leave it to me! I'll bring that girl back!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Aren't you suddenly cheerful now that you know we're not going to wrong park...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Starboard to going Merry!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Bring me up right next to her!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Aye- Aye.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Alright! The adventure about to unfold as I bravely step foot on these virgin lands,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: will hereby be known as "the warrior usopp's great adventure!"

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Fitting Title, Eh JohnnyBoy?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Aye- Aye.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Fishmen...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: full speed ahead.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Aye Aye.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: The hell are you just passing by for!?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: SSHH!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong Pirates
dialogue.text: Didn't you see them!? There were fishmen! The arlong pirates for crying out loud!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I'm freakin' scared out of my wits, you got a problem with that!? Do ya, punk!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The one who should be mad is me.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It's no good... this entire region seems to be under Arlong's control.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: What should we do, Brother usopp?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Well, now that we know we can't bring Nami back...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Just hurry up and untie me, you idiots!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Hmm, that's an unfamiliar ship!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Gahh!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Abandon ship!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Hey! Wait a sec!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Aye-aye!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: At least until me before you run away!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Heh heh, looks like I caught up to ya.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Stop the ship!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Huh, there's only one guy on- board...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Were ya exiled or sumthin'?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Yeah... sort of...

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: I swear I'll kill them for this!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I see, then those must be torture wounds?

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
